entries.definition:
gh–d–w time between the break of dawn and the rising of the
sun, to do things during such time; the morrow, future. Of this
root, five forms occur 16 times in the Qur’an: غَدَا ghadā three
times; غَد ghad five times; غُدُوّ ghuduww five times; غَدَاةٌ ghadātun
twice and غَدَاء ghadāʾ once.

غَدَا ghadā u [v. intrans.] to go in the early hours of the morning
(68:25) وَغَدَوْا عَلَى حَرْدٍ قَادِرِينَ they left early, bent on their purpose
[of depriving the poor].

غَد ghad I [n.] tomorrow, the future (59:18) وَلْتَنْظُرْ نَفْسٌ مَا قَدَّمَتْ لِغَدٍ
and let every soul consider carefully what it sends ahead for
tomorrow II [adv. of time] 1 tomorrow, on the day after today
(12:12) أَرْسِلْهُ مَعَنَا غَدًا يَرْتَعْ وَيَلْعَبْ send him with us tomorrow to run
about and play 2 in the future (31:34) وَمَا تَدْرِي نَفْسٌ مَاذَا تَكْسِبُ غَدًا
no soul knows what it will reap in the future [lit. tomorrow].

غُدُوّ ghuduww [v. n./n.] 1 the early hours *(24:36–7) يُسَبِّحُ لَهُ فِيهَا

661
